text.title: Rate Limiting Implementation
text.summary: FastAPI middleware pattern, tiered limits, endpoint-specific config, and standard response headers.

rule.statement: Always emit X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset, and Retry-After (on 429) on every rate-limited response. Also support the IETF draft RateLimit-* headers for modern clients.
rule.rationale: Standard headers allow clients to implement backoff without parsing error bodies. Missing Retry-After causes clients to retry immediately, generating more 429s and increasing load.

example.code:
from fastapi import FastAPI, Request from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
app = FastAPI()
@app.middleware("http") async def rate_limit_middleware(request: Request, call_next): user_id = request.headers.get("X-User-Id", request.client.host) key = f"rl:user:{user_id}"
allowed, remaining, reset_at = await rate_limiter.check(key) if not allowed: return JSONResponse( status_code=429, content={"error": {"code": "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED", "message": "Too many requests", "retryAfter": 60}}, headers={ "X-RateLimit-Limit": "100", "X-RateLimit-Remaining": "0", "X-RateLimit-Reset": str(reset_at), "Retry-After": "60", }, )
response = await call_next(request) response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] = "100" response.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"] = str(remaining) response.headers["X-RateLimit-Reset"] = str(reset_at) return response

rule.statement: Compose multiple keys per request: ip + user + apikey + tenant. Apply the limit on any one key being exceeded. This prevents a single compromised API key from bypassing IP limits and vice versa.
rule.rationale: Single-key limiters have blind spots. A CGNAT IP behind many users penalizes all of them. A leaked API key with IP rotation bypasses IP limits. Composing keys closes both gaps.

rule.statement: Apply X-Forwarded-For parsing with a trusted-proxy allowlist when the app is behind a CDN. Never use request.client.host directly — it will be the CDN's IP, limiting all traffic as one entity.
rule.rationale: Behind a CDN, request.client.host is always the CDN edge IP. All users share one rate limit counter, causing all traffic to be throttled simultaneously when any user hits the limit.

section.title: Tiered Limits
section.p: Different plans have different limits. Store limits in a config file or DB keyed by plan name. The limiter reads the applicable limit for the authenticated user's plan.
